name: What questions should I ask before hiring a door installer?

mainEntity.name: Which questions help me compare installers and avoid bad workmanship?
mainEntity.text: Which questions help me compare installers and avoid bad workmanship?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most useful hiring questions are the ones that reveal both product knowledge and installation quality. Ask whether the contractor is licensed and insured, who will actually perform the work, and whether the installers have experience with the specific door type you want. Then ask for a detailed written estimate that explains the scope of work, materials, timeline, warranty coverage, and cleanup. It is also smart to ask how they handle hidden rot, out-of-square frames, weatherproofing, and final adjustment visits after the door settles. For coastal Peninsula homes, ask whether they recommend corrosion-resistant hardware, better sealing, or moisture-resistant materials for your exposure level. Good installers answer directly and explain tradeoffs instead of giving vague reassurances. If a contractor cannot explain the installation process clearly, that is a warning sign.
acceptedAnswer.description: Ask whether the contractor is licensed and insured, who will do the work, what the warranty covers, how long the job will take, and whether the quote includes removal, sealing, cleanup, and final adjustments. Also ask if they have experience with the specific door type and local weather conditions. Clear answers usually signal a better installation.
